title: The FDA Ruled That ZYN Pouches Are Safer Than Cigarettes. That Doesn’t Mean They’re Safe
gist: The FDA granted Swedish Match a Modified Risk Tobacco Product order letting 20 ZYN nicotine-pouch products claim lower risk versus cigarettes, a regulatory validation arriving after a global pouch boom and amid WHO warnings about youth uptake.

insights.claim: On June 30 the FDA issued a Modified Risk Tobacco Product order authorizing 20 ZYN products—10 flavors at both 3mg and 6mg strengths—to claim lower risk of mouth cancer, heart disease, lung cancer, stroke, emphysema and chronic bronchitis versus cigarettes.
insights.speaker: Megan Tomos
insights.so_what: A first-of-kind US modified-risk endorsement is a marketing moat for Swedish Match and a template competitors must now chase to make comparable health claims.
insights.quote: using ZYN instead of cigarettes puts you at a lower risk of mouth cancer, heart disease, lung cancer, stroke, emphysema and chronic bronchitis.
insights.locator: On June 30, the US Food and Drug Administration

insights.claim: The FDA authorization is narrow: it covers only 20 specific products, applies solely to smokers who fully switch, excludes dual users of cigarettes or vapes, and advises non-tobacco users against pouches.
insights.speaker: Megan Tomos
insights.so_what: The tightly scoped claim limits marketing exposure and legal risk but also caps the addressable benefit narrative to complete switchers only.
insights.quote: First, the ruling only applies to 20 specific products, not all nicotine pouches. Second, the FDA’s findings only apply to smokers who fully switch to these pouches.
insights.locator: The new designations are a big win

insights.claim: The MRTP order is valid for five years unless renewed or withdrawn, and requires Swedish Match to track real-world usage and report back to the FDA.
insights.speaker: Megan Tomos
insights.so_what: A time-limited, surveillance-conditioned authorization means the claim can be revoked if youth uptake data turns adverse, a standing regulatory overhang.
insights.quote: The MRTP order is valid for five years, unless it is renewed or withdrawn.
insights.locator: Swedish Match must also keep track

insights.claim: Global retail sales of nicotine pouches reached 23.4 billion units in 2024, up more than 50 percent year over year, per a WHO report.
insights.speaker: Megan Tomos
insights.so_what: 50%+ annual unit growth marks pouches as the fastest-scaling nicotine category, a durable revenue tailwind for the tobacco majors that own the leading brands.
insights.quote: global retail sales of nicotine pouches reached 23.4 billion units in 2024, an increase of more than 50 percent from the previous year
insights.locator: According to a recent report by the World Health Organization

insights.claim: ZYN, On! and VELO are the global front-runners in oral nicotine pouches, but vapes and disposable e-cigarettes still hold the largest share of the smoking-alternative category, projected to hit $14.8 billion in the US by 2030.
insights.speaker: Megan Tomos
insights.so_what: Pouches are gaining but vapes remain the volume leader, so the alternatives market is fragmenting across pouches, vapes and heated tobacco rather than consolidating.
insights.quote: projected to hit $14.8 billion by 2030 in the US

insights.claim: The author frames the FDA ruling as regulators catching up to a cultural shift younger nicotine users already embraced, not a fresh blessing for the category.
insights.speaker: Megan Tomos
insights.so_what: If regulation is trailing demand, incumbents benefit from established distribution while late regulatory moves pose the main forward risk to the category.
insights.quote: In many ways, regulators are simply catching up with a cultural shift that already happened.
insights.locator: It’s the first time regulators have formally endorsed

insights.claim: The WHO warns pouches are spreading faster than regulation can keep pace, with brands using social media, influencers and youth-oriented marketing, and flags 'strong youth appeal and high addiction potential' among adolescents and young adults.
insights.speaker: Megan Tomos
insights.so_what: Youth-uptake evidence is the primary vector for future crackdowns, mirroring the vape backlash and the key tail risk investors should monitor in the category.
insights.quote: strong youth appeal and high addiction potential
